title: OSAC UI Flow Spec
description: Formal schema for ui-flows/*.yaml files — describes user-visible journeys, step states, transitions, RBAC gates, and validation rules for the OSAC demo application.

flow.description: Human-readable name for this flow, matching the title in FLOW-INDEX.yaml flows[].

statechart_ref.description: Relative path to the companion statechart YAML file.

rbac.description: RBAC gates for each step in this flow.

required_permissions_by_step.description: Map of canonical step ID → map of action label → required permission string.

additionalProperties.description: Map of action label → permission name.

steps.description: Ordered list of UX steps (screens, modals, or logical waypoints) in this flow.

transitions.description: Directed edges describing how the user or system moves between steps across flows.

validation_rules.description: Client-side field validation constraints for this flow. Empty array is valid when there are no constraints.

applies_when.description: Optional prose describing the RBAC / session conditions under which this flow is active (e.g. role and tenant context).

terminology.description: Optional glossary of domain terms, display-name clarifications, or structured content tables specific to this flow. Values may be strings (simple glossary entries) or structured objects/arrays (e.g. os_families table).

routing_matrix.description: Optional list of role/persona routing choices available on the welcome landing. Used only in welcome-and-role-selection.

branding_profiles.description: Optional per-tenant branding for institutional-sign-in when using sign_in_shell.

pre_login_document_titles.description: Optional mapping of tenant context to document.title values shown before authentication.

pre_login_theme_defaults.description: Optional per-tenant dark/light theme applied before sign-in.

role_preconditions.description: Optional map describing which branding profile and pre-filled identifier apply per persona after welcome routing. May include a top-level 'description' string.

description.description: Optional prose overview of this section.

Step.description: A single UX waypoint — a screen, modal, drawer, or logical state within the flow.

id.description: Globally unique canonical step ID (see FLOW-INDEX canonical_step_ids). Prefix matches the flow (e.g. shell_, auth_, tud_, mvm_).

title.description: Short human-readable label for this step.

goal.description: One-sentence description of what the user achieves in this step.

ui.description: PatternFly / logical component names rendered in this step.

components.description: List of logical component names from FLOW-INDEX reusable_components_across_flows or PF primitives used directly.

notes.description: Optional prose notes for implementers describing design intent, icon usage, or composition patterns not expressible in the components list.

states.description: Discrete UI states this step can be in (mirrors statechart states).

interactions.description: Human-readable list of user actions or system behaviours available in this step.

role_variants.description: Optional per-role presentation differences (sidebar nav, masthead, fleet scope). Used in application-shell-session.

entry_context.description: Optional map of named entry paths into this step with a prose description of each. Used in institutional-sign-in.

UiPolicy.description: Optional implementation policy for UI composition (e.g. prefer PatternFly primitives over raw wrappers).

prefer_patternfly_primitives.description: When true, prefer PatternFly layout/composition components for structure.

minimize_raw_div_wrappers.description: When true, avoid structural raw div containers unless no practical PF equivalent exists.

max_raw_div_wrappers.description: Cap on raw opening <div> tags (without pf-primitive-exception) in TSX that implements this step. Enforced by pnpm check:pf-primitives: each file's leading block comment must include flow: <kebab-flow-id> matching docs/specs/ui-flows/<flow>.yaml, and optionally step: <id> or steps: id1, id2; otherwise the checker uses the max budget across all steps in that flow. Non-div raw tags are not covered by this number (they always require pf-primitive-exception). See design-system.yaml layout_and_shell.implementation_policy.automated_check_alignment.

avoid_custom_css.description: When true, prefer BEM block CSS (osac-* co-located with components as PascalCase .css files) and PF layout primitives instead of inline style props. Chart SVG height wrappers and global scrollport rules remain exceptions per design-system.yaml.

StepContent.description: Optional static copy and content structure for a step (used on the welcome landing).

Transition.description: A directed edge from one step to another, triggered by an action.

from.description: Canonical step ID of the source step (may be in this flow or another).

action.description: Human-readable description of the user or system event that triggers this transition.

to.description: Canonical step ID of the destination step (may be in this flow or another).

browser_target.description: Optional — whether the transition opens in the same tab or a new tab.

ValidationRule.description: Client-side validation constraint for a specific field in this flow.

field.description: Name of the field or logical input being validated.

when.description: Optional condition under which this validation applies (e.g. 'deployment equals new').

rules.description: One or more named validation constraints.

note.description: Optional prose explaining prototype behaviour or QA expectations.

EntryBootstrap.description: Describes optional query-param bootstrap and related navigation rules for welcome and sign-in.

query_param.description: URL query parameter name used to bootstrap tenant and role on page load.

browser_history.description: How SPA routing interacts with the browser history stack (e.g. Back after welcome → sign-in).

allowed_values.description: Exhaustive list of recognised query param values.

unknown_value_behavior.description: Prose description of how the app behaves when an unrecognised value is provided.

RoutingChoice.description: One row in the welcome routing matrix — maps a user card/button choice to browser target, tenant, role, and navigation destination.

choice.description: Label of the card or button the user clicks (e.g. 'Provider Admin — Enter').

tenant_id.description: Internal tenant identifier set in app state (e.g. vertexa, northstar, evergreen).

role.description: Demo shell role applied (e.g. providerAdmin, tenantUser, tenantAdmin).

next_step.description: Canonical step ID the user lands on in this tab after the choice.

then.description: Canonical step ID of the subsequent step (same tab, after next_step).

osac_entry.description: Equivalent osac-entry slug for optional deep links or bookmarks; welcome card clicks use same-tab session state instead of this query param.

SignInShell.description: Single React shell implementing institutional sign-in; branding_profiles supply per-tenant configuration.

component.description: Name of the React component implementing auth_sign_in_form for all institutions.

BrandingProfile.description: Per-tenant branding and persona scope for the institutional sign-in shell.

id.description: Internal identifier for this profile (e.g. vertexa_provider).

tenant_id.description: Stable tenant key used by session routing (vertexa | northstar | evergreen).

display_name.description: Human-readable institution name shown in UI.

personas.description: Prose describing which demo personas use this profile.

RoleColumn.description: Tenant role column on the welcome landing (tenant_admin or tenant_user).
